reasoning: While the programmatic text metrics show high recall, the LLM visual fidelity score is critically low at 0.5 due to the failure to render essential map imagery and agency logos. The document contains major visual defects including maps appearing as solid green blocks and redundant heading duplication, which prevents auto-approval under the cost-asymmetry principle.

concerns.analysis: The core visual elements of the document—the maps—are failed or missing. The map on Page 1 (showing LiDAR phases) is completely absent from the HTML output. The maps on Pages 2 and 3 are represented only by large solid green rectangles, which fails to convey any of the geographic or project data contained in the source maps. Additionally, the DPS and EMAP logos present in the source are missing from the HTML rendering.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF contains complex maps on every page: a phase map of North Carolina on page 1, and topographic refresh maps of Western NC on pages 2 and 3. It also includes DPS and EMAP logos in the header and footer areas.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML screenshot shows no image for the Page 1 map. For the maps on Pages 2 and 3, there are only solid green rectangular blocks. The DPS and EMAP logos are missing.
concerns.location: Throughout the document; specifically where maps should appear for each page section.

concerns.analysis: The headings for the third section of the document are visually duplicated. The HTML output renders the page title and subtitle once as a combined heading and then immediately repeats them as two separate heading elements, creating redundant visual noise and organization issues.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 3 title: '2025 Data Collection Area' and subtitle 'North Carolina Collaboratory'.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML shows three consecutive headings: '2025 Data Collection Area North Carolina Collaboratory', '2025 Data Collection Area', and 'North Carolina Collaboratory'.
concerns.location: HTML output, section corresponding to Page 3.

action_items: Restore or correctly embed the map images on pages 1, 2, and 3.
De-duplicate the redundant heading elements in the third section.
Ensure agency logos are properly captured and include appropriate alt-text.
